libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For tests covering com.mchange.v2.naming.ReferenceableUtils.referenceToObject(...) overloads that do not take an explicit allowedFactoryClassNames Set, or ReferenceIndirector.getObject(), set the system property com.mchange.v2.naming.objectFactoryWhitelist to the fully qualified test ObjectFactory class name(s), comma-separated, and restore/clear it after the test. Alternatively pass a PropertiesConfig containing that key or call an overload with an explicit allowed Set. Use local JNDI names such as java:comp/env/... unless deliberately testing name-guard rejection; non-local names may require targeted configuration of com.mchange.v2.naming.nameGuardClassName.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #2097 [Automation] java run fails for com.mchange:mchange-commons-java:0.4.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: A broad or stale whitelist can hide the security behavior introduced in 0.4.0; keep values limited to test ObjectFactory classes.
System properties are JVM-global, so tests should restore previous values to avoid order-dependent failures.
Optional integrations exist for Typesafe Config, log4j, log4j2, and slf4j, but they are not required for meaningful core utility/JNDI coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: mchange-commons-java 0.4.0 added mandatory JNDI ObjectFactory whitelist checks; tests that dereference javax.naming.Reference via convenience overloads fail unless the whitelist is configured.
